title: Brief (machine-checkable extract)
description: JSON sidecar for units/<UID>/brief.md. Gates the PLUMBING of the executor contract (ids, deps, budget, criteria, tags, applied learnings, the socratic PROTOCOL REFERENCE) NOT the prose reasoning, which stays free-form in brief.md. Reason free-form, extract the residue downstream: validity != correctness. The ANSWERED socratic block lives in debrief+verify (the canonical 4-key block); the brief only carries a reference to the protocol. Adds tags (from V_tag) + learnings_applied for the learnings-propagation predicate.

socratic_protocol.description: REFERENCE to the Socratic protocol the executor MUST run before output (e.g. 'references/socratic-protocol.md'). The brief mandates the protocol; the ANSWERED 4-key socratic block is required in debrief.schema + verify.schema, never here (reason free-form, extract the residue downstream).

tags.description: This unit's tags, drawn from the enumerated vocabulary V_tag declared in graph.schema.json (`v_tag`). Membership (tags subset of V_tag) is enforced by validate_run.py. Basis for the tag-scoped learnings-propagation predicate.

learnings_applied.description: Ids of LEARNINGS entries applied in this unit. MAY be empty. The validator's propagation predicate REQUIRES that any tag-scoped learning E whose tag this unit carries (and created no later than this unit's wave) appears here. `L#` = a run/project-scoped entry; `G#` = a store-imported global entry, listable here once it is re-grounded/active (I12 force-injects re-grounded G# entries) — the pattern mirrors learnings.schema.json's `^[LG][0-9]+$` id.

dod_refs.description: OPTIONAL (guardrail enforcement). Mirror of the graph unit's `dod_refs` — the definition_of_done items this unit serves, each VERBATIM ∈ clarifications.json definition_of_done. When the graph unit carries dod_refs, validate_run.py I20's brief-mirror clause requires this sidecar to carry the SAME set (sorted-equality); NOT in `required` so archived runs stay schema-valid.

non_goal_refs.description: OPTIONAL (guardrail enforcement). Mirror of the graph unit's `non_goal_refs` — the non_goals items this unit must actively respect, each VERBATIM ∈ clarifications.json non_goals. MAY be [] (explicit 'no non-goal applies' — no minItems; empty-STRING elements still barred). Same I21 brief-mirror posture as dod_refs.
